steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The narrator states directly that he “could not remember what [Radha Aunty] looked like” and then immediately goes to the corridor to look at family photographs. This cause-and-effect sequence reveals his purpose.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The narrator is trying to recover a memory—he wants to see what his aunt actually looks like. Choice C — ‘He hopes the photographs will help him recall what his aunt looked like.’ matches this exactly: he hopes the photographs will help him recall (bring back to mind) what she looked like.

The other choices introduce unsupported elements—writing a story, interest in his father's clothing, and communicating something to his aunt—that contradict the passage's actual focus on the narrator's personal effort to remember.

That confirms C is correct.
